description: If your vehicle breaks down on a road, use hazard lights and move off the road if possible.
When a bus signals to leave a bus stop in a built-up area, you must give way if the speed limit is 70 km/h or less.
The driver entering the road from a driveway or car park must give way to vehicles already on the road.
You must carry your driver licence or be able to produce it when requested.
When passing stationary emergency vehicles with flashing lights, slow down and proceed with caution.
A flashing red traffic light requires you to stop and proceed when safe.
A flashing yellow traffic light means you may proceed with caution.
Do not drive through floodwater if depth or safety is uncertain.
When approaching a give way sign, you must slow down and give way to all conflicting traffic.
At an uncontrolled intersection, you must give way to vehicles approaching from your right.
A green left arrow at traffic lights means you may turn left without giving way.
You must use headlights between sunset and sunrise or when visibility is poor.
If your indicators fail, use hand signals where possible and proceed cautiously.
Learner drivers must display L-plates at all times when driving to inform other road users of their learner status.
You may only legally use a mobile phone while driving if the vehicle is legally parked.
Driving on a nature strip is illegal unless crossing it to access a driveway.
The overtaking driver is responsible for ensuring it is safe to overtake.
When directed by a police officer to stop, you must stop as soon as it is safe.
At a railway level crossing with flashing red lights, you must stop and wait until the lights stop flashing.
At a red traffic light, you must stop and remain stopped until the light changes.
When both vehicles are turning right at an intersection, neither vehicle must give way if their paths do not cross.
When entering a roundabout, give way to vehicles already in the roundabout.
When two vehicles arrive at a roundabout at the same time, the vehicle entering must give way to the vehicle on the right.
When entering traffic from a shoulder, the driver entering traffic must give way.
When approaching an intersection with a stop line, you must stop completely at the line.
When approaching a stop sign, you must come to a complete stop and give way to all traffic.
At a T-intersection with no signs, the driver on the terminating road must give way.
You are permitted to drive across a painted traffic island when it is broken-lined and safe to do so.
If a traffic sign conflicts with a road marking, follow the traffic sign.
When making a U-turn, the vehicle making the U-turn must give way to oncoming vehicles.
You may make a U-turn at traffic lights if it is safe and legal to do so.
